codeReview.summary: The feature broadly implements the W3 trend view requirements (range presets, resolution toggle, raw dots + smoothed line, baseline bands, trend indicators, loading/empty states), but there is a blocking date-axis bug in chart timestamp conversion.

issues.description: Chart points are timestamped with `new Date(d.date).getTime()` where `d.date` is `YYYY-MM-DD`. In JavaScript this is parsed as UTC, so users in negative timezones see the plotted day shifted backward (e.g., `2026-03-28` renders as Mar 27 in America/New_York). This makes trend dates inaccurate in the core visualization. Use local-date parsing (e.g., `parseISO(d.date)`) consistently for timestamp generation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared docs do not currently call out a date-only parsing convention for frontend charts, which allowed a UTC/local-time parsing bug to slip into a core visualization path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md contain no frontend date parsing rule; implementation uses `new Date(d.date).getTime()` at src/components/dashboard/TrendDetailView.tsx:362 with date-only API strings.

summary: Reviewed feature trend-view-page using handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit diff. Marked FAIL due to a blocking timezone/date parsing issue that shifts trend chart days for non-UTC users.
